instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Why is the Renewal of your Term Insurance Plan Important?
Meta Description:  The significance of term life insurance renewal cannot be overstated. Continue reading to know more about it!
Keywords: term insurance renewal, term insurance, term life insurance
________________________________________________________
Introduction
You plan and take appropriate efforts, such as saving and investing your money in the correct channels, to reduce various risks in life and protect yourself and your family from financial problems. In this light, term life insurance is a wise investment that attempts to protect your family financially if you are unable to do so.
When does my Term Insurance end
A term insurance policy's goal is to financially recompense your loved ones in the event of your untimely death within the term insurance plan's duration. Your term insurance coverage will expire if you do not pay your payments on time, leaving your family without financial protection while you are gone. Therefore, when you buy term insurance plan, be sure to keep it current and renew it on time.
Why is it important to renew your Term Insurance
The following are some of the consequences of not doing your term insurance renewal:
Undermines the basic objective of insurance - Term insurance is purchased to provide a financial safety net for your loved ones in the event of your death. In the case of your death, the term insurance plan pays out the amount promised as a benefit to your family. However, if you do not pay your term insurance payments and do not renew your policy on time, you are putting your family's financial stability at risk.
Termination or decrease of term plan benefits - If your term insurance policy fails due to non-payment of premiums, your family will not receive a death benefit or any extra benefits provided by riders. All previously paid insurance premiums will be useless as a result. Furthermore, if you do not renew your term insurance policy within a certain period of time, the insurance company may require you to pay interest in order to reinstate your coverage.
Higher rates for new term insurance plans - If you do not renew your term insurance plan on time, your policy will lapse, and you will be required to pay a higher price if you get term insurance later. You'll also have to pay late premium interest, which will raise the overall cost of your term plan even more. Furthermore, when you renew your term insurance policy, your rates stay the same. You won't be charged anything more, and you'll just have to fill out a few forms. However, due to changes in medical condition, age, and other factors, you will pay a different and probably higher premium if you get term insurance for the first time.
Renewed waiting time - A new term plan with a critical illness benefit may have a higher waiting period, which varies by product. You must start again if you do not renew your term insurance coverage on time.
No tax advantages - Section 80C of the Income Tax Act of 1961 allows for tax savings of up to Rs 1.5 lakh on term plan premiums. These benefits, however, are only available if your term insurance policy is current. You cannot claim the tax advantages for the year if your insurance fails before maturity due to non-payment of premiums since you have not paid the premium. As a result, your taxable income rises, and you must pay more taxes.
Wrapping Up
Aside from these disadvantages, the non-renewal of a term insurance policy results in further complications. You must repeat the medical examinations as well as the KYC checks. This implies that purchasing term life insurance will take longer than just renewing your existing term insurance policy. You save time, money, and taxes, be sure to do term insurance renewal on time and keep it current.
Check out Bajaj Allianz Life’s insurance options to provide a secure financial safety net for your loved ones.
Sources:
https://www.pnbmetlife.com/articles/protection/why-renewal-of-term-insurance-policy-is-equally-important.html

output.final_text:
Why is the Renewal of your Term Insurance Plan Important? Meta Description: The significance of term life insurance renewal cannot be overstated. Continue reading to know more about it! Keywords: term insurance renewal, term insurance, term life insurance ________________________________________________________ Introduction You plan and take appropriate efforts, such as saving and investing your money in the correct channels, to reduce various risks in life and protect yourself and your family from financial problems. In this light, term life insurance is a wise investment that attempts to protect your family financially if you are unable to do so due to some unfortunate event. When does my Term Insurance end? A term insurance policy's goal is to financially recompense your loved ones in the event of your untimely death within the term insurance plan's duration. Your term insurance coverage will lapse if you do not pay your premiums on time and even after passing of the grace period provided by the insurance company to remit the premium payment, leaving your family without financial protection while you are gone. Therefore, when you buy a term insurance plan, be sure to keep it active and renew it on time. Why is it important to renew your Term Insurance? The following are some of the consequences of not doing your term insurance renewal:
● Undermines the basic objective of insurance - Term insurance is purchased to provide a financial safety net for your loved ones in the event of your death. In case of your death, the term insurance plan pays out the amount promised as a benefit to your nominees. However, if you do not pay your term insurance premium and do not renew your policy on time, you are putting your family's financial stability at risk. Termination or decrease of term plan benefits - If your term insurance policy lapses due to non-payment of premiums, your family will not receive a death benefit or any extra benefits provided by riders, if opted for, as per rider terms and conditions. All previously paid insurance premiums will be useless as a result. Furthermore, if you do not renew your term insurance policy within a certain period of time, the insurance company may require you to pay interest in order to reinstate your coverage.
● Higher rates for new term insurance plans - If you do not renew your term insurance plan on time, your policy will lapse, and you may be required to pay a higher price if you get term insurance later. You may also have to pay late premium interest, which will raise the overall cost of your term plan even more. Furthermore, when you renew your term insurance policy, your rates stay the same.. However, due to changes in medical condition, age, and other factors, you will pay a different and probably higher premium if you get new term insurance plan later in life rather than renewing your existing policy. ● Renewed waiting time - A new term plan with a critical illness benefit may have a higher waiting period, which varies by the kind of ailment as well as terms and conditions of the product. You must start again if you do not renew your term insurance coverage on time.
● No tax advantages - Section 80C of the Income Tax Act of 1961 (the Act) allows for tax savings of up to Rs 1.5 lakh on term plan premiums subject to the provision stated in the Act. These benefits, however, are only available if your term insurance policy is active. You cannot claim the tax advantages for the year if your policy lapses before maturity due to non-payment of premiums. As a result, your taxable income rises, and you must pay more taxes. Wrapping Up Aside from these consequences, the non-renewal of a term insurance policy may result in further complications. You must repeat the medical examinations as well as the KYC checks. This implies that purchasing term life insurance may take longer than just renewing your existing term insurance policy. You save time, money, and taxes, and hence it is preferable to do term insurance renewal on time and keep the policy active. Disclaimer: The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
